semanticCore: Spoils of war, optional prayer, extra gift, bonus, or grandchild
primaryMeaning: Spoils of war or optional gift/prayer
quranicRelevance: Spoils of war (Anfal - 'ani al-anfali'), grandson given as extra gift (Nafilah - 'wa ya'qooba nafilatan'), and optional night prayer ('nafilatan laka').

keyDerivations.meaning: Spoil of war, booty, extra distribution, or gift
Extra gift, optional prayer/deed, supererogatory act, or grandson

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَمِنَ اللَّيْلِ فَتَهَجَّدْ بِهِ نَافِلَةً لَكَ (17:79): And from the night, pray therewith as an extra/optional worship for you (prophetic night distinction).
summaryEn: The root represents extra allocations, covering military spoils of war (Anfal), Zechariah's grandson Jacob given as an extra gift (Nafilah), and night prayers.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الزيادة على الفريضة والهبة الإضافية، واصفاً غنائم الحرب والأنفال، ووهب يعقوب حفيداً نافلة لإبراهيم، وصلاة قيام الليل النافلة.
rawText: نَافِلَةٌ : the pl. نَوَافِلُ, is explained in the TA, art. حرز, by زَوَائِدُ [Accessions, or additions]. === What accedes to, or exceeds, the original. (T.) A voluntary gift, by way of alms, or as a good work: (T:) a gift: (K:) or a gift عَنْ يَدٍ: (M:) a deed beyond what is incumbent, or obligatory. (M, K.) === نَافِلَةٌ Supererogatory prayer. (S, Msb.) See تَطَوَّعَ. نَفَلٌ Trifolium melilatus indica of Linn.: and medicago intertexta of Linn. (Delile, nos. 706, 730.) === نَفَلٌ: see غَنِيَمَةٌ. نَفَّلَهُ 2 , inf. n. تَنْفِيلٌ, He gave him spoil, (S, Msb, * K,) and a free and disinterested gift. (Msb, K.) And it is doubly trans.: see 2 in art. غنم. نُفَلٌ : see تُسَعٌ.
